2603.23857.title: When AI output tips to bad but nobody notices: Legal implications of AI's mistakes
2603.23857.paper_summary: The adoption of generative AI across commercial and legal professions offers dramatic efficiency gains -- yet for law in particular, it introduces a perilous failure mode in which the AI fabricates fictitious case law, statutes, and judicial holdings that appear entirely authentic. Attorneys who unknowingly file such fabrications face professional sanctions, malpractice exposure, and reputational harm, while courts confront a novel threat to the integrity of the adversarial process. This failure mode is commonly dismissed as random `hallucination', but recent physics-based analysis of the Transformer's core mechanism reveals a deterministic component: the AI's internal state can cross a calculable threshold, causing its output to flip from reliable legal reasoning to authoritative-sounding fabrication. Here we present this science in a legal-industry setting, walking through a simulated brief-drafting scenario. Our analysis suggests that fabrication risk is not an anomalous glitch but a foreseeable consequence of the technology's design, with direct implications for the evolving duty of technological competence. We propose that legal professionals, courts, and regulators replace the outdated `black box' mental model with verification protocols based on how these systems actually fail.
2603.23857.paper_summary_zh: 商業和法律職業中生成式人工智慧的採用帶來了顯著的效率提升——然而，對於法律而言，它引入了一種危險的失效模式，在這種模式下，人工智慧虛構了看似完全真實的案例法、法規和司法判決。無意中提交這些虛構內容的律師面臨專業制裁、職業失誤的風險以及名譽損害，而法院則面臨對對抗性程序完整性的全新威脅。這種失效模式通常被視為隨機的「幻覺」，但最近基於物理學的分析揭示了Transformer核心機制中的一個確定性成分：人工智慧的內部狀態可以跨越可計算的閾值，導致其輸出從可靠的法律推理轉變為聽起來權威的虛構內容。在這裡，我們在法律行業的背景下呈現這一科學，通過一個模擬的簡報草擬場景進行說明。我們的分析表明，虛構風險並不是一個異常的故障，而是該技術設計的可預見後果，對技術能力的演變責任有直接影響。我們建議法律專業人士、法院和監管機構用基於這些系統實際失效方式的驗證協議來取代過時的「黑箱」心理模型。
2603.23857.author: Dylan J. Restrepo et.al.
2603.23857.authors: Dylan J. Restrepo, Nicholas J. Restrepo, Frank Y. Huo, Neil F. Johnson
